metric_value: Up to 50% of capital expenditure is now for IT
Object technology becomes a requirement 3-5 years out
If MIS is the competitive advantage of the 1990s, mainframes do not provide a differentiator. New mainframes are not good long-term investment.

Consolidate data centers; Cheap MIPS - used or non-IBM; Outsource - particularly static applications; Freeze/lower MIS budgets; Downsize with replicated systems; Non-MIS divisional/departmental installations

Simply put, the flip side of the new super-low-cost computing units being delivered by the new computing industry is a huge integration task — but also a tremendous business opportunity. The smart companies are laying the foundation now for doing this kind of integration work.
